followUps.title: Migrate downstream consumers of acronyms-catalog from file/name refs to D9 stream URL
followUps.scope: 5 downstream consumers identified in T1 substrate Step 7: schema-registry/schemas/patent/glossary.schema.json — update $ref to https://schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.json; LuhTech-business/filings/glossary.md + glossary.yml — link to stream URL; LuhTech-business/docs/LUHTECH-PATENT-FOUNDATION-CORRECTIONS-2026-05-06.md — update reference; LuhTech-business/.roadmap/follow-up-register.json — close stale catalog-absence FU entries.
followUps.coordination: Ectropy-Business follow-up-register should also note this as a cross-repo concern once D9 stream URL is validated live.

followUps.title: Resolve cosmetic jsonschema deprecation warning for decision-log DEC validation
followUps.scope: Step 17 of T2 Phase 1: DEC log validation passes but triggers jsonschema network auto-fetch deprecation warning. Root cause: decision-log.schema.v2 $refs luhtech-enums; the Draft7Validator may not propagate the local registry through to internal sub-validator $ref resolution. Audit referencing.Registry propagation in validate.py; confirm luhtech-enums $id coverage in schema-map; consider explicit pre-resolution walk.
followUps.coordination: Likely folds into T3 substrate prep — fixing propagation lets T3 instance validations run clean. Cosmetic only; validation passes.

resolution.summary: Resolved without code change. Root cause was substrate-ordering during T2 Phase 1: schema-map.json had not yet been generated when DEC log validation ran in Step 17, so the local registry was empty and luhtech-enums $refs fell through to jsonschema network auto-fetch. T2 landed the schema-map; T3 substrate Step 7 reproduced validation under post-T2 registry and observed zero warnings. Lesson: regenerate indices BEFORE running dependent validations.
